howItWorks.intro: This body recomposition calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Individual results vary based on metabolism, genetics, and activity level. Consult your doctor before starting any program.

faq.question: What is body recomposition?
faq.answer: Losing fat and building muscle simultaneously. Best suited for beginners, those returning to training, or people with higher body fat. You eat at maintenance or a slight deficit with high protein.

faq.question: How much protein for recomp?
faq.answer: 1.0-1.2g per pound of lean body mass is optimal. This is higher than for simple weight loss because you need protein for both fat loss and muscle building.

faq.question: Is recomp slower than cut/bulk?
faq.answer: Yes — the scale may not change much since muscle gain offsets fat loss. Track progress with measurements, photos, and strength gains rather than scale weight.

faq.question: Can experienced lifters recomp?
faq.answer: It is harder for advanced trainees. They may benefit more from traditional bulk/cut cycles. Beginners and intermediates respond best to recomposition.

faq.question: Is the Body Recomposition Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Body Recomposition Calculator?
sections.body: The Body Recomposition Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes and fitness enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the fitness and training space. By entering your weight, body fat, activity, you get instant results including daily calories, protein, carbs. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting daily calories right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In fitness and training, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes and fitness enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to optimize your training with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your weight and need to find the right daily calories. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Weight (lbs)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your weight in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Body Fat (%)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your body fat in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Activity (1=Sedentary 2=Light 3=Moderate 4=Active)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your activity in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your daily calories, protein, carbs. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your weight before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both daily calories and protein — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to optimize your training.
If you're unsure about your activity, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
